response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished senior full-stack and web developer with strong software craftsmanship, but the role requires deep hardware-adjacent expertise — SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appears in the resume. His low-latency DLang work and brief C/C++ IDE tooling experience are the closest touchpoints, but they fall far short of principal-level computer architecture and deep-learning workload optimization experience.
strengths: Strong systems-level performance experience with low-latency, high-throughput services (async I/O, fibers, caching) at Sociomantic
Broad technical range across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded, with 15+ years of professional experience satisfying the BS + 8 years path
Demonstrated craftsmanship and knowledge-sharing: TDD, open-source libraries, conference talks, and workshops
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon architecture, or hardware simulation experience of any kind
No AI/deep-learning workload, accelerator, GPU, or SIMD/MIMD architecture background
Career trajectory is web/product engineering, not performance architecture — a principal-level hardware role would be a large domain leap

key_factors.factor: No deep-learning workload or GPU experience
